pageDescription: Hill censer, boshanlu. Made of earthenware. glazed in green, crackled, and partially incrusted with iridescence. The cover is shaped like conventional hills with jagged rocks, and perforated to emit incense fumes; round the base is a band of moulded lozenge trellis enclosing raised dots.
extractedText:
boshanlu | British Museum
Hill censer, boshanlu. Made of earthenware. glazed in green, crackled, and partially incrusted with iridescence. The cover is shaped like conventional hills with jagged rocks, and perforated to emit incense fumes; round the base is a band of moulded lozenge trellis enclosing raised dots.
Object Type: boshanlu
Museum number: 1912.1007.3
Description: Hill censer, boshanlu. Made of earthenware. glazed in green, crackled, and partially incrusted with iridescence. The cover is shaped like conventional hills with jagged rocks, and perforated to emit incense fumes; round the base is a band of moulded lozenge trellis enclosing raised dots.
Cultures/periods: Han dynasty
Production date: 1stC-2ndC (circa)
